titleStmt.title: SII 15.26: original edition by P. B. Desai, Dines Chandra Sircar and G. S. Gai
No. 26. (B.K. No. 115 of 1926-27.) HOSŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On two slabs built into a wall (left of entrance) of the Trikūṭēśvara temple.

This is dated the 7th regnal year of the king, Raktākshi, Chaitra-pūrṇimā Sunday, lunar eclipse. The details of this date, which is identical with that of No. 25 above correspond to 1144 A.D., July 16, assuming Chaitra to be a mistake for Śrāvaṇa.

It states that Nāmiyaṇṇa-nāyaka who was the Sarvādhikāri of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Hemmāḍidēva of the Kāḷasēna family and Haṭṭagāralineage, made a gift of the income derived from the cess on the betel leaves called bīravaṇa, for burning a perpetual lamp before the god Bhōgēśvara. The gift was entrusted to Jñānaśaktipaṇḍita, the āchārya of the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.note: This inscription is engraved in continuation of B.K. No. 114 ofSouth Indian Inscriptions Vol., XI, Pt. ii, No. 152.
div.p:
© svasti samastapraśastisahitaṁ śrīmanmahāmaṁḍaḷēśva
raṁ kāḷasēnavaṁśōdbhavaṁ haṭṭagāṟakulakumudinīniśākaraṁ hēmmāḍidēvarasara sarvvādhi kāriyappa nāmiyaṇḍanāyakaru dharmmacittarāgi svasti śrīmacāḷukyapratāpa cakravartti jagadēkamalladēvavarṣada
7 neya raktākṣi saṁvatsarada caitra śuddha paurṇnamāse Ādivāra sōmagrahaṇadaṁdu śrī bhōgēśvaṁdēvaṁ naṁdādēvigaṁdalliyācārya�ppa jñānaśaktipaṇḍitadēvarakālaṁ� karcci tavage naḍeva bīravaṇamaṁ dēvara tōṁṭadeleye
nitu hēṟādoḍamanitakkaṁ baḷiyele sahitavāgi hēṟigoṁdu paṇamaṁ biṭṭaru .
Digital edition of SII 15.26 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
